recentTweets.text: The UK just made backing the IRGC a felony. Better late than never, I suppose. On today's @FDD Morning Brief, I ask @RobinSimcox why this designation is coming now and what it means for Tehran's reach in Britain. https://t.co/7zUmYOezAe
Yeah, except Greece, Cyprus, Israel, Armenia, and Syria.

I’m old enough to remember when people called Gaza an open air prison before the war.

I’m also old enough to remember that Hamas launched the war.
The United Nations stopped delivering aid to millions of Yemenis nine months ago. No one seems to care. https://t.co/neUGbEvIy4

Hamas:

1. Not disarming
2. Slaughtering its opponents
3. Not returning bodies

Trump needs to come down like a ton of bricks on our “friends” in Doha and Ankara. 

Qatar & Turkey are Hamas’s patrons and protectors. If the deal fails, it’s on them.
Qatar reportedly firing the top echelon of Al-Jazeera execs. Waiting for official confirmation
Reminder: Half of London is owned by Qatar
Hands down the most 2025 headline I have seen in 2025. https://t.co/3jR8UI9QiT

10 hostages over 5 different handovers?

I can’t believe we’re still playing Hamas’ game.

Squeeze the hell out of Qatar & Turkey, Hamas’s top patrons, and get them all out —all 50, alive & dead - at once. 

Every last one needs to be released on the same day. Then end the war.
As part of his capitulation to Israel and America, Ali Khamenei should be forced to call upon Hamas to release every last hostage, dead and alive.

From an impeccable Israeli contact:

The strike appears to have ended the Iranian nuclear program. Confirmation to come in the next 24 hours. The last step is to remove the nuclear material from the nuclear sites.

October 7, 2023 was the day the Iranian regime believed Israel would begin to collapse.

June 21, 2025 was the day the regime finally understood that it had made a terrible mistake.
This is a strange tweet, given that it is now an established fact that Hamas operates command centers under Palestinian hospitals in Gaza.
Beware of media outlets that try to tell you how to think. https://t.co/tzwTN3Foqh

Israelis bracing for as many as 2000 ballistic missiles from Iran tonight. If the regime  uses them all, they’ll have nothing left. If they don’t, Israel will destroy them. Use or loose. Regardless, they will have to reckon with the Israeli Air Force tomorrow. Tensions high.

Finally.

Netanyahu calls upon Qatar to stop playing both sides in hostage talks.

I’d love to see the same from the US side now. https://t.co/8VW2WttDtv
As someone who has studied Hamas since the 1990s, I tend to disagree. https://t.co/Mr0nymzEzV

Turkey’s foreign minister meeting with Hamas. 

Explain to me why we want to allow Turkey back into the F-35 program

The entire Arab world will go apoplectic over Trump's  proposed takeover of Gaza. And it's not hard to understand why. But here's the thing: Trump doesn't need to do anything after this but wait. The very fear of a this will finally get the Arab world to move on the "day after."

Yahya Sinwar must be turning in his grave. 

The war he launched:

1. Left Gaza in rubble
2. Destroyed Hamas
3. Hammered Hezbollah
4. Toppled Assad
5. Left Iran without air defenses
6. Could even lead to loss of Palestinian control of Gaza
State sponsor of terror
Qatar’s Major Non-NATO ally status truly must be revoked. This is ridiculous.

Jimmy Carter used to give annual town hall lectures at Emory. I felt the need to attend them as a student. But as I began to understand the depths of his toxic anti-Israel post-presidency, I actually refused to give a penny to Emory so long as he was affiliated with the school.
“We did it, my brother. We got Al-Qaeda installed as the new rulers of Syria.”
Surprise!

I know Trump has said he wants the US to stay out. But this WH and those set to join the next admin should be warning Turkey and Qatar to keep their henchmen away from Israel’s border and away from the Kurds or there will be terrible consequences.

To recap:

Turkey facilitated the rise of ISIS

Turkey helped Iran evade sanctions 

Turkey supports Sunni jihadis, including Al-Qaeda, in Syria.

Turkey now hosts the Hamas HQ

Turkey bought a Russian s-400 system in violation of its NATO obligations 

Turkey is a rogue state.

One thing I want to stress about last night’s operation was the sheer complexity. 

1/3 or maybe even 1/2 of the Israeli Air Force was in the sky last night, striking enemy targets 1000 miles from home. 

All the aircraft and pilots came back without a scratch.

No simple feat.

Some of Hamas’s top surviving leaders live in Qatar.

Qatar must put those Hamas leaders under house arrest.

Qatar must force them to surrender on Al-Jazeera.

Qatar must force them to call for the release of all remaining hostages.

That’s how the Gaza war ends, ⁦@POTUS⁩. https://t.co/Vs7ISqo171

*IF* Sinwar is dead, here's what needs to happen:

When Qatar-based Hamas leader Khaled Meshal very likely takes over the organization, the US needs to put massive pressure on Doha.

Qatar should be forced to freeze Meshal's assets and then extradite him  to the US or Israel...
Now debunked by US officials. Embarrassing that this was posted without casting a shred of doubt.

Hezbollah has launched more than 240 rockets at Israel today on Rosh Hashana.

I’m old enough to remember when the international community put immense pressure on Israel to not advance on southern Gaza during the entire month of Ramadan.

Reports tonight out of Lebanon tonight that Israel targeted Osama Hamdan, one of the few remaining identifiable Hamas leaders in Lebanon.

This is just remarkable to watch. Nearly every leader of the two main Iran proxy groups I’ve followed for nearly 3 decades: eliminated.
Watching the Israelis systematically take out Hezbollah’s top leaders in short order with apparent relative ease makes me wonder: why didn’t they do this sooner?

Turkey should never acquired the S400.

Turkey should never have been in the F35 program.

Turkey should not be in NATO.

The US needs to get out of Incirlik.
So, this just happened. https://t.co/oU26rlwJ0u

Khaled Meshal just called upon Hamas to resume suicide bombings against Israel. 

Khaled Meshal lives in Qatar.

Qatar is a purported US ally. 

It's time for Qatar to extradite Meshal.

A new piece from me & @FDD colleague @NatalieEcanow in @Commentary.

https://t.co/IwaxHaCM1J

Americans will wake up today & learn that Hamas murdered another 6 Israeli hostages, including one American-Israeli whose story was highlighted at the DNC.

Some thoughts to follow that will *not* focus on the heartbreak/trauma in Israel.

Let’s focus on who bears responsibility.

Southern Gaza is where the IDF and Shin Bet recovered a hostage alive today.

Southern Gaza is where the Biden Administration repeatedly told Israel not to operate.

Southern Gaza is where the Egyptian regime has repeatedly told Israel not to operate.

Israel is getting attacked on 7 fronts by the world’s top state sponsor of terror.

Biden doesn’t get it: The more he pressures Israel, the more Iran steps up its aggression. 

The moment he throws his weight behind Israel & snarls at Iran is the moment this thing de-escalates.
Hamas without Ismail Hanieyah and without Mohammed Deif is not the Hamas that existed before 10/7.

I can't overstate this: 

The Middle East is now potentially hours away from the most devastating war in its bloody history.

A Israel-Hezbollah war would likely yield a shocking number of fatalities and destruction.

The US knew this day would come. Our leaders have failed.

A few hours ago, an El Al flight had to make an emergency landing in Antalya, Turkey.

Reports from Israeli channel 12 now conveying that Turkish authorities are not allowing the plane to take off again. 

The Erdogan government is effectively holding an entire plane hostage.

I keep coming back to one detail from the news this morning. 

Hamas fired at the hostages as they were being rescued.

To bring everyone up to speed:

On the same morning that IDF reports finding 3 dead hostages under an UNRWA facility, and amidst continued reports of Hamas-UNRWA cooperation, the ICJ rules against Israel… 

…citing UNRWA reports.

You can’t make this stuff up.
Nawaf Salam, ICJ judge, is citing UNRWA reports as evidence right now. You can’t make this up.

Turkey now treating 1,000 wounded Hamas terrorists in country.

Question: how are they getting there?

If you told me after 10/7 that American students would be protesting on behalf of the killers & rapists, I wouldn’t have believed you.

If you told me that Biden would withhold military assistance to Israel in its battle with Hamas, I’d have told you to stop sniffing glue.

Never mind that Biden just threw Hamas a lifeline. 

He just demonstrated to every country in the Middle East and beyond that an alliance with America is worth little to nothing during election season. 

Who in their right mind would seek out this kind of an alliance?

🧵Campus protest thread here.

I’ve received lots of calls & emails in recent days related to this testimony from 2016.

https://t.co/LAf94Br1fS

I testified again last year on the same topic

https://t.co/pwmNwFP9DD

Both about a group called American Muslims for Palestine.

Gaza Health Ministry Cannot Provide Names for More Than 10,000 It Says Have Died. 

@fdd’s ⁦@adesnik⁩ is not having any of Hamas’s math. https://t.co/j2PniPOSq5
Convicted Palestinian Islamic Jihad financier:
Columbia needs to start kicking kids out of school and checking IDs to keep agitators off campus. What am I missing here?
A reminder, kids. When someone does something nice, say “thank you.”
Saudi official says the quiet part out loud: Iran engineered war in Gaza to ruin normalization with Israel https://t.co/iNh9bx7Jdz

Talking to European friends in various government positions, there has been an overnight shift in views on the Middle East. 

After recoiling from the images out of Gaza for weeks (even months), there is now wide recognition that this entire thing has been engineered by Iran.

British planes from Cyprus knocked a bunch of Iranian drones out of the sky tonight. US systems nailed a bunch. Jordanian systems did, too. And of course Israel.

Not a bad night for the Western-led alliance in the Middle East. Not bad at all.
Break off these terrible alliances
Tough to grasp why people are losing faith in mainstream media.
IDF confirms: Hamas #2 military commander Marwan Issa has been killed.

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 4 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Jonathan Schanzer. The social-media posture reads as security-first / counterterrorism frame because the strongest matched frame is security and counterterrorism language. Top matched terms: Syria / Rojava, Kurds / Kurdish.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around security and counterterrorism language. I count 1 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hit, 2 security-frame hits, 0 supportive or diplomatic hits, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 0 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2025-11-05T12:26:16Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

The photo on the right is from Ramallah in 2000. 

Aziz Salha proudly waved his bloody hands from the window as an Israeli was lynched.

Salha was arrested then released in a  hostage deal that released…Yahya Sinwar.
If only there was a multilateral legal body out there that investigated calls for genocide.

Qatar now arguing against Israel at the ICJ.

At the same time, it pretends to play a neutral role in Paris — with the lives of the Israeli hostages in the balance.

Remember: Qatar has provided a headquarters & $30 million a month to Hamas.

Qatar is a state sponsor of terror.

Qatar says it got permission from Hamas to send medications to the hostages.

The last batch of medicine was found in the trash by Israeli soldiers.

This is all Qatari theater.

Qatar is a Hamas patron pretending to help.

Qatar's PM finally comes out with it. He calls for an immediate ceasefire, regardless of whether the hostages are released. 

There you have it, folks.

Qatar never cared about releasing the hostages. 

Qatar wants the war to end to preserve their client Hamas in Gaza.

Crazy stuff out of Rafah:

A Gazan boy was shot dead by Hamas after he tried to access humanitarian goods. A riot broke out. Hamas then opened fire to contain the rioters.

https://t.co/mjUByDWbNt

I clearly struck a nerve yesterday. So let me repeat:

Qatar helped create this crisis by funding Hamas, and giving the group a HQ in Doha.

Qatar could force the Hamas to surrender & release the hostages. It not done so.

We must hold Qatar responsible when the war ends.

I’ll say this again: 

Qatar helped create this mess by funding & harboring Hamas.

Qatar could wield its leverage with Hamas to force the group to surrender & release the remaining hostages. It hasn’t.

Qatar must be held to account when this war ends.

Hakan Fidan, the former Turkish Intell chief who built a Hamas HQ in Turkey, attended ⁦@SecBlinken⁩’s meeting on Gaza’s future.

What the actual f*ck is going on at ⁦@StateDept⁩? 

This is beyond bad judgment. Why not just ask the Supreme Leader of Iran to join? https://t.co/1jO6B9d260
More than 150 Hamas fighters surrendered today

IDF says it has found 800 Hamas tunnels in Gaza so far. 500 destroyed. 

Crazy how much time, effort and money went into this war infrastructure. 

Equally crazy to ponder: how much of that time, effort and money could have gone into building Gaza for the Gazan people.
It’s rare that a social media post captures the desperation of a corrupt organization that knows it cannot exist much longer. Here is one such post:

Iran and Thailand have a direct hostage channel.

This is quite telling.

Iran is so clearly the sponsor of Hamas that Thailand went right to the source of power.
53 hostages to be released. 40 kids and 13 mothers. This appears final. Deal imminent.

If Qatar somehow manages to deliver on some of the hostages, it should get no credit.

This tiny dirt patch of a country has funded and sheltered Hamas for more than a decade. 

Qatar created this crisis, along with Iran and Turkey. 

Qatar must be held to account.
Just waiting for all the retractions and apologies from all those who denied Shifa's role in Hamas military infrastructure.
Israeli strike on tunnels in Northern Gaza (Beit Hanoun). Watch how the explosion extends in multiple directions.

Qatar-based Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh is now in Turkey after traveling to Iran. 

Reminder: Iran, Qatar and Turkey are the 3 primary jurisdictions of Hamas terror operations around the world. 

Why are Qatar and Turkey not named state sponsors of terrorism by @StateDept?
Hamas now firing rockets out of southern Gaza, which is where Israel has tried to facilitate a humanitarian effort. Hamas is, once again, using the  civilian population as a tool in this war.

Now that the Ashli hospital flap is over, the media must prepare for a story that will be difficult to navigate. The Al-Shifa hospital in Gaza City is also the command center for Hamas. At some point, it will be an Israeli target. A legitimate military target, per the laws of war

Qatar has failed to secure the release of the hostages. In other words, the reason that it cites for sustaining a Hamas headquarters — leverage over the group— was a lie. The same goes for Turkey. Their status as state sponsors of terror should be declared as US policy.

I repeat: Hakan Fidan was the intelligence chief who presided over the establishment of a Hamas headquarters in Turkey that included an active terrorist commander directing attacks in the West Bank, Saleh al-Arouri.

Out of curiosity, I just glimpsed the timelines of Amnesty, HRW & ACLU to see their response to the attack on Salman Rushdie. As it turns out, they didn’t respond at all. Not one tweet on the attack against an intellectual, inspired by a rogue state. What happened to these NGOs?

The narrative that ISIS is hardcore while the Taliban is pragmatic is flat out bonkers, reflecting a total lack of understanding of the landscape. Both organizations are jihadist. Both embrace violence and seek America’s destruction. Both are American enemies.
Channel 13 reporting that IDF has given a green light to targeting *all* Hamas operatives at *all* levels, both inside Gaza and out.  Obviously implications for Iran, Turkey, Qatar, Malaysia, etc.
